title: [PyCDE] Move to richer module/instance ops
body:
PyCDE currently uses `hw.instance` and `hw.module`. Since we are adding concepts on top, this has become unwieldy as we've added more and more:
- PyCDE adds properties to them using misc attributes.
- There are methods whose correct home is in the instance or module op, but they are not general enough to add to `hw.instance`/`hw.module`. 
- We need to represent parameterized modules which been declared, but not generated.
- We need to represent parameterized modules which have been generated for a particular set up parameters.

As such, we need to add design-level instance/module ops. Since the additional concepts are not PyCDE specific (PyCDE is just a very opinionated binding), we'll be adding them in the `msft` dialect initially. (That dialect is basically a playground for us at Microsoft.)

TODOs:

- [x] Create `msft.instance`
- [x] Lower `msft.instance` to `hw.instance`
- [x] Create `msft.module`. Attributes: parameterization values, port info, and symbol name.
- [x] Lower `msft.module` to `hw.module`
- [x] Create `pycde.module` python classes containing the python binding classes.
- [x] Have PyCDE create `msft.instance` instead of unregistered ops. Create a new (bodyless) `msft.module` for each different parameterization.
- [x] Re-write generator to target bodyless `msft.module`s and generate the bodies. When the generator is re-written, do it entirely in python.
